name: Theory/Optimization · Condition-Result-Regime Variant
full_intro_template: P1 Problem statement: We study <task> under explicit assumptions <A1, A2>.
P2 Formal gap: Existing methods provide weak guarantees in regime <R>.
P3 Intuition: We connect <phenomenon> with <theoretical mechanism> to explain failure/success.
P4 Main idea: Introduce <principle> enabling tighter guarantees or better trade-off.
P5 Theorem roadmap: State theorem scope, assumptions, and practical implication.
P6 Lower-bound/barrier: Clarify impossibility or hardness boundary where applicable.
P7 Empirical alignment: Show how theory predicts behavior in controlled experiments.
P8 Contribution summary: theorem, algorithmic implication, and diagnostic evidence.
abstract_template: A1: Define task and deployment setting with one measurable constraint.
A2: State precise gap and why prior work fails in that regime.
A3: Present method/protocol and strongest baseline delta under matched budget.
A4: Add uncertainty (mean±std/significance) and one limitation boundary.
method_template: M1 Define notation, assumptions, and objective formally.
M2 Present key lemma and proof strategy overview.
M3 Derive main theorem with regime and constants disclosure.
M4 Translate theory into implementable algorithmic steps.
M5 Complexity and convergence or sample-efficiency analysis.
M6 Practical notes for reproducible implementation.
experiments_template: E1 Controlled experiments validating theorem predictions.
E2 Comparison with strongest practical baselines.
E3 Sensitivity to assumption violations and boundary cases.
E4 Ablation linking each theoretical component to empirical effect.
E5 Statistical reliability and uncertainty reporting.
E6 Efficiency trade-off under matched budgets.
falsifiable_claims: If <core module> is removed, performance on <hard subset> should decrease by >= <delta>.
Under matched training/inference budget, <our method> should outperform <strong baseline> on <metric>.
delta_vs_strongest_baseline: Compared with <strong baseline>, we add <mechanism> and obtain <delta> under matched budget and protocol.
limitations_or_failure_cases: We explicitly analyze failure cases under <scenario> and report why the method is not dominant there.
contribution_schema: Contribution: <type> | Evidence: <verifiable artifact> | Risk/Boundary: <where it may fail>

operational_definition.B: resource or reliability metric reported jointly

paragraph_cards.template: P1 Problem setup with concrete operational constraints.
paragraph_cards.evidence_hook: Link this paragraph to a concrete artifact: Table/Figure/Theorem/Ablation.

anti_pattern_examples.bad: We achieve SOTA on all benchmarks.
anti_pattern_examples.better: Under matched training/inference budgets, we outperform strong baselines on <metric> over <subset>.

anti_pattern_examples.bad: This is the first work to study <task>.
anti_pattern_examples.better: To the best of our knowledge, this is one of the first unified treatments under <assumptions>.
We quantify stability on <stress test> and report failure cases on <hard subset>.

statistical_reliability_requirements: Report mean±std over >=3 random seeds when stochastic training is used.
State whether significance testing was performed and which test was used.
cost_disclosure_requirements: Report at least one cost metric from: compute complexity, memory, runtime.
Compare cost under matched hardware/batch settings.

reorder_profiles.description: context -> gap -> idea -> evidence -> limitation -> contributions
context -> evidence -> gap -> idea -> limitation -> contributions
context -> protocol -> gap -> idea -> evidence -> contributions

conclusion_template: C1 Summarize the theoretical contribution and practical meaning.
C2 Clarify valid regime and known limitations.
C3 Point to open problems and broader applicability.
